task_completion.description: Locate and select the 'press_power' input for configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing an inputs list or input configuration page with 'press_power' visible and selected for editing.

task_completion.description: Configure a processing chain to convert the power draw into a binary status (0 or 1).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the processing chain/process list for 'press_power' showing conditional logic, thresholds, or mathematical operations designed to output 0 for low values (~25W) and 1 for high values (~1500W).

task_completion.description: Log the binary output to a feed named 'press_running'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a 'Log to feed' step in the processing chain targeting 'press_running', or the feeds list displaying the 'press_running' feed.

task_completion.description: Create a new dashboard named 'Factory Monitor'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a dashboard interface, dashboard list, or dashboard editor with the title 'Factory Monitor'.

task_completion.description: Add an LED widget to the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'Factory Monitor' dashboard showing an LED widget (typically a circular light/indicator icon) placed on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Link the LED widget to the 'press_running' feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the LED widget's configuration panel or properties showing it is explicitly connected to the 'press_running' feed.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's graphical user interface to configure the inputs, feeds, and dashboards.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web-based UI (like Emoncms) rather than terminal commands, API scripts, or database direct edits.

integrity.description: The dashboard was created within the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard interface matches the application's built-in dashboard editor, not a local HTML file, text editor, or image drawing tool.

integrity.description: The agent used an actual LED widget rather than faking the output with static text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The widget on the dashboard is a genuine UI component for an LED indicator, not a static text box simply displaying 'press_running: 1' or an uploaded static image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input name must be exactly 'press_power'. The output feed must be exactly 'press_running'. The dashboard must be exactly 'Factory Monitor'. The processing chain must contain logic to differentiate between standby power (~25W) and active power (~1500W) to output a binary 0 or 1. The dashboard must contain an LED widget linked to the 'press_running' feed.
